fair_value_today.basis: Daedalus v3.0 SOTP argued base — the anchor, not a weighted blend

fair_value_12m.basis: #1 rolled forward at r=8.1% less dividend 0.0

detail.multiple_rationale: 14.5x FY2027E EBITDA ($554M) as the defensible 12-month traded multiple. Rationale: (1) LEAN TOWARD BULL on G&A synergies — $10M of $15M Mid-Atlantic G&A savings is CONTRACTED (Stifel post-investor-summit June 10 confirmed 'thesis intact'; Goldman Sachs conviction buy list add June 8 citing 'synergy realization as central value driver' — these are observable, near-term, and program-specific, not hoped-for); (2) LEAN AGAINST FULL MULTIPLE CONVERGENCE in 12 months — 14.5x is still 4.5x below RSG (17x) and 1.5x above current 13.0x, reflecting that Highland permit (Q1 2027) has not yet been in hand and the full integration story requires 18-24 months to play through, meaning the market will not fully re-rate to 15x+ in one year; (3) Cross-reference: CWST's own 5-year median EV/EBITDA is 14.8x, achieved at lower (20-23%) margins — with 24.2% FY2027E margins, 14.5x is below historical average, leaving upside. The gap between #3 (intrinsic convergence, $114.11) and #4 (traded price target, $108.60) represents the portion of the call that rests on the market keeping the 15x exit premium — this is explicitly named as the market risk, not assumed away.

street_reconstruction.what_price_implies: At 14.0x FY2026E Adj. EBITDA ($B), the current price implies a ~9.6% FCF CAGR for 10y (then 2% terminal) at r=8.0%.
street_reconstruction.what_street_implies: The street mean target implies ~12.4% for 10y.
street_reconstruction.history_anchor: CWST 5-year median EV/EBITDA ~14.8x; peaked at 19-20x in 2021-2022 on M&A optionality premium; compressed to 13-14x in 2025-2026 on rate sensitivity and acquisition dilution
street_reconstruction.contested_lever: EBITDA margin re-rate: whether CWST closes the 7-10pp gap to large-cap peers (WM/RSG 28-32%) through M&A integration synergies ($15M G&A target) and Highland/Hakes permit-enabled internalization, or whether the perpetual acquisition pace permanently suppresses blended margins below 26% and caps the multiple re-rating the bull thesis requires

conviction.label: MEDIUM
conviction.basis: action BUY: Daedalus assigned MEDIUM (evidence read across base/bull/bear) -> MEDIUM

sensitivity_matrix.lever_1_name: SW EBITDA margin at FY2030 normalization (the crux lever)
sensitivity_matrix.lever_2_name: Exit EV/EBITDA multiple for Solid Waste at maturity
sensitivity_matrix.note: RS EV held at $507M (9.5x $53.4M). Net debt $1.115B. Shares 63.7M. Interim FCF PV $350M included in SW EV. SW revenue $2.12B at FY2030 (base path). Each cell is the implied stock price in USD.
sensitivity_matrix.dominant_lever: EXIT MULTIPLE dominates per unit (each 2x step = ~$12-13/share); MARGIN is the crux because it is the MORE UNCERTAIN lever. At base 15x multiple, moving margin from 24% to 26% = $7.50/share. At base 27% margin, moving multiple from 15x to 17x = $13/share. The call hinges on MARGIN (the crux) because the multiple range has a defensible anchor (historical median 14.8x); the margin range is genuinely contested.

base_cell.note: Manual calc without interim FCF PV; engine base with $350M interim FCF PV = $110.99

sensitivity_matrix.current_price_implied_zone: At $86.98, the market prices either 22-24% FY2030 margin at 15x, or 24-26% margin at 13x — both reflecting no margin re-rate thesis and/or GFL-style integration-discount multiple. Our base (27% margin, 15x) produces $105.60 ex-interim-FCF or $110.99 with it.
sensitivity_matrix.fragility_finding: The call is NOT fragile to the exit multiple at 13x-15x (stock is already cheap relative to our base at both levels). The call IS fragile to margin: if margin stagnates at 22-24% (bear case), the stock is fairly valued at $61-80 at the base multiple (13-15x). If margin reaches 26-28% (above base but below bull), the stock is worth $75-109 even at a conservative 13x, and $100-123 at 17x. The bull case is WELL above current price; the bear case at $56.50 is below. This is an asymmetric setup skewed bullish, consistent with the BUY rating.

bridge_logic: #1 fair value today 110.99 -> #3 fair value 12m 119.94 (x(1+0.081) - div 0.0); #4 target 12m 108.6 (defensible_multiple); implied return to #4 = 24.9%, to #3 = 37.9%, to street mean 112.0 = 28.8%
